page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the c4a server family. It is equipped with 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory. This allocation provides a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not include integrated GPU hardware or local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage.

Operating with dedicated CPU allocation, the c4a-standard-16 ensures consistent compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. While benchmark data is not available for this specific configuration, the dedicated resource model is designed to deliver predictable execution times for multi-threaded applications. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a balanced resource density, making it a cost-effective option for workloads that require steady CPU performance without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.

The resource profile of the c4a-standard-16 makes it suitable for a variety of general-purpose and moderately compute-intensive workloads. Appropriate use cases include web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, and medium-sized database hosting where local SSD storage is not a strict requirement.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Part of the c4a server family, this instance maintains a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. With dedicated CPU allocation, the instance delivers predictable performance free from resource stealing. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring stable, continuous compute power without the overhead of specialized hardware. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and CI/CD pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. It offers a balanced 1:4 resource ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-16 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, designed for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine with dedicated compute resources and a stable one-to-four processor-to-memory ratio for general-purpose workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
